columns.extraction_plan: The total number of participants in the control arm (ADT arm) is 193. This is explicitly stated in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2: "From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm." This is further confirmed in Table 1 on page 3, which lists "ADT (n = 193)".

The add-on treatment is Docetaxel. This is explicitly stated in the title of the article: "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone...". It is also mentioned in the abstract under "Design, setting, and participants" (page 1, Chunk 4): "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients...". Further confirmation is found in the "Patients and methods" section (page 2, Chunk 4): "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."

The control arm is referred to as "ADT alone" or simply "ADT" throughout the document. For example, on page 1, in the "Design, setting, and participants" section, it states: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients; median follow-up of 7 yr." On page 2, in the "Patients and methods" section, it further clarifies: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone." Tables 1 and 2 also use "ADT" to denote this arm.
Extract the median value from the "Age, yr, median (IQR)" row under the "ADT plus D (n = 192)" column in Table 1. The value is 63.0.
Extract the median value from the "Age, yr, median (IQR)" row under the "ADT (n = 193)" column in Table 1. The value is 64.0.

The column is not reported in the PDF. The document does not provide a formal 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence' for the trial itself, nor does it use terminology associated with such an assessment (e.g., GRADE or Cochrane Risk of Bias ratings).

The trial name is reported in Chunk 4, on page 1, in the title of the article. Extract "GETUG-AFU15 Trial". It is also mentioned in the abstract and body of the text.

The author list is reported in Chunk 4, on page 1, at the beginning of the article. This is a full publication. The document contains multiple pages of detailed methods, results, and discussion sections, which is characteristic of a full publication.
The phase is reported in Chunk 4, on page 1, in the title and abstract. Extract "Phase 3".

The study is a follow-up analysis. In Chunk 4, on page 1, the abstract states: "to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study." Also, in Chunk 4, on page 2, under "Introduction", it mentions "updated survival analyses." Extract "Follow Up".

The number of arms included is 2. This information is found in Chunk 4, on page 1, in the "Design, setting, and participants" section of the Abstract, which states: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone". It is also confirmed in Chunk 4, on page 2, in the "Patients and methods" section: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D... or ADT alone."

The "Results" section specifies that 192 patients were in the "ADT plus D arm" (page 3, Chunk 5). As per the trial design, all patients randomized to the ADT plus D arm were intended to receive docetaxel. Therefore, 192 patients (100%) in the treatment arm received docetaxel.

The "Results" section states: "In the GETUG-AFU15 study, in contrast, the vast majority of patients (79%) in the ADT arm received so-called salvage D within a median time of 18.5 mo (95% CI, 2.9-179)" (page 5, Chunk 5). The total number of patients in the ADT arm was 193 (page 3, Chunk 5). Calculating 79% of 193 gives approximately 152 patients (0.79 * 193 = 152.47).
The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for patients in the ADT plus D arm (treatment arm) in the overall population is reported as 22.9 months.
The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for patients in the ADT arm (control arm) in the overall population is reported as 12.9 months.
The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for patients with high-volume disease (HVD) in the ADT plus D arm (treatment arm) is reported as 15.2 months.
The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for patients with high-volume disease (HVD) in the ADT arm (control arm) is reported as 9.2 months.
The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for patients with low-volume disease (LVD) in the ADT plus D arm (treatment arm) is reported as 40.9 months.
The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for patients with low-volume disease (LVD) in the ADT arm (control arm) is reported as 22.4 months.
Primary end point was OS. (from the "Article info" section, under "Outcome measurements and statistical analysis").

The Quality of Life Scale used in the GETUG-AFU15 trial is not reported. While "quality of life" is mentioned in the discussion (Chunk 5, page 5) in a general context of future studies and in reference [26] (Chunk 5, page 6) referring to a different trial (GETUG 12), there is no mention of a specific scale or questionnaire used to assess quality of life as an outcome in the GETUG-AFU15 study itself.

The value is reported as "62.1" in Table 2, page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, row "ADT plus D, mo", column "All patients". It is also stated in the Abstract on page 1 and in the "Overall survival" section on page 2: "62.1 mo (95% CI, 49.5–73.7) in the ADT plus D arm".

The value is reported as "48.6" in Table 2, page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, row "ADT, mo", column "All patients". It is also stated in the Abstract on page 1 and in the "Overall survival" section on page 2: "48.6 mo (95% CI, 40.9–60.6) for ADT arms".

The value is reported as "39.8" in Table 2, page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, row "ADT plus D, mo", column "HVD". It is also stated in the Abstract on page 1 and in the "Overall survival" section on page 2: "39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0–53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9–43.6) (HR: 0.78 [95% CI, 0.56–1.09]; p = 0.14), for low-volume disease (LVD) patients; median was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5-NR) and 83.4 mo (95% CI, 61.8-NR) (HR: 1.02 [95% CI, 0.67-1.55]; p = 0.9)."

The value is reported as "35.1" in Table 2, page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, row "ADT, mo", column "HVD". The value is reported as "83.4" in Table 2, page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, row "ADT, mo", column "LVD". It is also stated in the Abstract on page 1 and in the "Overall survival" section on page 2: "83.4 mo (95% CI, 61.8-NR)".

The document mentions "quality of life" in the context of the GETUG 12 trial (a different trial) in reference [26] on page 6, but it does not report quality of life results for the GETUG-AFU15 trial, which is the subject of this paper. Not reported. While 'radiographic progression' is defined, the percentage of patients with progressive disease as an initial outcome is not reported. The median duration of the entire on-treatment therapy (Androgen Deprivation Therapy [ADT] plus Docetaxel [D]) is not reported as a single median value in months. The paper states that patients in the GETUG-AFU15 study's ADT plus D arm received a "median number of eight... cycles of D" (page 5, text, "Discrepancies between the GETUG-AFU15 and the CHAARTED studies cannot be explained by treatment exposure because patients received a median number of eight and six cycles of D, respectively"). While this provides the median duration for the docetaxel component (approximately 8 cycles * 21 days/cycle = 168 days, or about 5.5 months), the ADT component of the treatment is continuous, and a combined median duration for the entire regimen is not provided.

The median duration of on-treatment therapy for the control arm (Androgen Deprivation Therapy [ADT] alone) is not reported. ADT is typically a continuous therapy, and the paper does not provide a specific median duration in months for how long patients in this arm received ADT. While median overall survival (OS), biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS), and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) are reported, these are measures of time to an event, not necessarily the duration of on-treatment therapy.

The secondary endpoints are biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS). This information can be found in Chunk 4, on page 1, in the "Abstract" section under "Outcome measurements and statistical analysis". It is also reiterated in Chunk 4, on page 2, in the "Patients and methods" section under "2.1. Statistics".

The total number of participants is 385. This value is reported in the "Abstract" section on page 1, stating "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients". The treatment arms are "ADT plus D" (Androgen Deprivation Therapy plus Docetaxel) and "ADT alone" (Androgen Deprivation Therapy alone). This information is explicitly stated multiple times. On page 1, in the Abstract, under "Design, setting, and participants": "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients; median follow-up of 7 yr." On page 2, in the "Introduction" section: "The GETUG-AFU15 trial... assessed the benefits of the addition of D to ADT in patients with mNCPC..." On page 2, in the "Patients and methods" section: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."

Table 1 on page 3 also includes a row for "Metastases at diagnosis, n (%)".
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with metachronous disease. This is indicated in the text on page 4, section 3.1 "Overall survival", which states: "For patients with metastases after failure of local treatment, median OS was not reached (95% CI, 69.5–NR) in the ADT plus D arm and was 69.8 mo (95% CI, 62.2-NR) in the ADT arm (HR: 0.83 [95% CI, 0.47-1.47]; p = 0.5) (Supplementary Table 2)." Table 1 on page 3 also includes a row for "Metastases after treatment for local disease, n (%)".
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with high volume disease (HVD). This is indicated in the Abstract on page 1, which states: "Median OS in ADT plus D and ADT arms, respectively, was for HVD patients: 39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0–53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9–43.6) (HR: 0.78 [95% CI, 0.56–1.09]; p = 0.14)". Table 2 on page 4 provides "Survival data... by treatment arm and metastatic burden" including a column for "HVD". Table 1 on page 3 also includes a row for "High-volume disease (%)".
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with low volume disease (LVD). This is indicated in the Abstract on page 1, which states: "for low-volume disease (LVD) patients; median was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5-NR) and 83.4 mo (95% CI, 61.8-NR) (HR: 1.02 [95% CI, 0.67-1.55]; p = 0.9)". Table 2 on page 4 provides "Survival data... by treatment arm and metastatic burden" including a column for "LVD". Table 1 on page 3 also includes a row for "Low-volume disease (%)".
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume

columns.extraction_plan: The OS rate for the ADT plus D arm at the latest follow-up (8 years) can be calculated from Figure 1 on page 3. The table below Figure 1 shows that 32 patients were still at risk at 8 years for the ADT plus D arm, out of an initial 192 patients. The rate is (32 / 192) * 100 = 16.67%.

The OS rate for the ADT arm at the latest follow-up (8 years) can be calculated from Figure 1 on page 3. The table below Figure 1 shows that 33 patients were still at risk at 8 years for the ADT arm, out of an initial 193 patients. The rate is (33 / 193) * 100 = 17.10%.

The PDF reports median OS for patients with metastases at diagnosis (synchronous) in the ADT plus D arm (52.6 months) in the text on page 4, section 3.1, but does not provide a specific OS rate (%) at the latest follow-up.

The PDF reports median OS for patients with metastases at diagnosis (synchronous) in the ADT arm (41.5 months) in the text on page 4, section 3.1, but does not provide a specific OS rate (%) at the latest follow-up.

The PDF reports median OS for patients with metastases after failure of local treatment (metachronous) in the ADT plus D arm (not reached) in the text on page 4, section 3.1, but does not provide a specific OS rate (%) at the latest follow-up.

The PDF reports median OS for patients with metastases after failure of local treatment (metachronous) in the ADT arm (69.8 months) in the text on page 4, section 3.1, but does not provide a specific OS rate (%) at the latest follow-up.

The OS rate for high-volume disease (HVD) patients in the ADT plus D arm at the latest follow-up (8 years) can be calculated from Figure 2 on page 3. The initial number of HVD patients in the ADT plus D arm is 92 (from Table 1, page 3). The table below Figure 2 shows that 11 patients were still at risk at 8 years for the ADT plus D arm (HVD). The rate is (11 / 92) * 100 = 11.96%.

The OS rate for high-volume disease (HVD) patients in the ADT arm at the latest follow-up (8 years) can be calculated from Figure 2 on page 3. The initial number of HVD patients in the ADT arm is 91 (from Table 1, page 3). The table below Figure 2 shows that 10 patients were still at risk at 8 years for the ADT arm (HVD). The rate is (10 / 91) * 100 = 10.99%.

The OS rate for low-volume disease (LVD) patients in the ADT plus D arm at the latest follow-up (8 years) can be calculated from Figure 3 on page 4. The initial number of LVD patients in the ADT plus D arm is 100 (from Table 1, page 3). The table below Figure 3 shows that 21 patients were still at risk at 8 years for the ADT plus D arm (LVD). The rate is (21 / 100) * 100 = 21.00%.

The OS rate for low-volume disease (LVD) patients in the ADT arm at the latest follow-up (8 years) can be calculated from Figure 3 on page 4. The initial number of LVD patients in the ADT arm is 102 (from Table 1, page 3). The table below Figure 3 shows that 23 patients were still at risk at 8 years for the ADT arm (LVD). The rate is (23 / 102) * 100 = 22.55%.

The treatment classes for the two arms of the trial are "Hormonal therapy" and "Chemohormonal therapy". This classification is explicitly used in the discussion section when referring to the CHAARTED trial, which had a similar design.
